summary: Warp a source Image/Field by a per-pixel Field2 displacement: output (x, y) reads the source at (x + 0.5 + dx, y + 0.5 + dy) with the M1 bilinear/nearest sampler and an explicit boundary mode. Zero displacement is identity; a constant displacement is a translation.

inputs.name: source
inputs.doc: The raster to warp (an Image, Mask, Sdf, or Field). Its descriptor and extent are preserved on the output.

inputs.name: displacement
inputs.doc: The per-pixel (dx, dy) lookup offset in physical pixels; must share the source extent.

outputs.name: warped
outputs.doc: The warped raster, same descriptor/extent as `source`.

params.name: filter
params.doc: The reconstruction filter (M1 resize vocabulary): nearest or bilinear.

params.name: boundary
params.doc: Out-of-bounds policy { mode: constant|transparent|clamp|mirror|wrap, value?: N }; absent is clamp.

perceptual.reason: field.domain_warp is a displacement-field resampler verified by analytic properties (zero-displacement identity, constant-displacement integer/fractional translation equivalence, warp/inverse-warp round-trip within the bilinear tolerance, boundary-mode honoring); there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
